panels.spec/description: The percentage of the time during which Scylla utilized the CPU. Note that because Scylla does busy polling for some time before going idle, CPU utilization as seen by the operating system may be much higher. Your system is not yet CPU-bottlenecked until this metric is high.
Amount of requests served as the coordinator. Imbalances here represent dispersion at the connection level, not your data model.

The percentage of the time during which Scylla utilized the CPU. Note that because Scylla does busy polling for some time before going idle, CPU utilization as seen by the operating system may be much higher. Your system is not yet CPU-bottlenecked until this metric is high.
Amount of requests served as the coordinator. Imbalances here represent dispersion at the connection level, not your data model.

Represents the ‘load’ value the balancer equalizes across instances.

It is based on the number of tablets assigned to each instance, adjusted so larger disks are expected to hold proportionally more tablets.

scylla_load_balancer_load

panels.spec/vizConfig/spec/options/content: Reads and Writes - Coordinator

panels.spec/description: Foreground writes are writes that weren't acknowledged yet to the application. For instance, if a single replica responded and two are needed due to the consistency level. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased write latencies.

Background writes are writes that are already acknowledged to the application but have additional work to be done. For instance, if a replica responded and only one is needed, this request is still listed as a background request until all replicas respond. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased write latencies.

Foreground reads are reads that weren't acknowledged yet to the application. For instance, if a single replica responded and two are needed due to the consistency level. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased read latencies.

Background reads are reads that are already acknowledged to the application but have additional work to be done. For instance, if a replica responded and only one is needed, this request is still listed as a background request until all replicas respond. This metric represents a queue size, not a rate. High values here correlate with increased read latencies.

Number of unexpected errors during sending, sending will be retried later

scylla_hints_manager_send_errors

Number of times a digest read was done on behalf of a speculative retry.

Speculative retry is a mechanism that causes the client or server to speculate that a request may fail, and send a new request.

speculative retry may reduce latency in exchange for system load, but only if there is little activity.

A lot of speculative retries increases load and can harm latency more than helping.

Number of times a read was done on behalf of a speculative retry.

Speculative retry is a mechanism that causes the client or server to speculate that a request may fail, and send a new request.

speculative retry may reduce latency in exchange for system load, but only if there is little activity.

A lot of speculative retries increases load and can harm latency more than helping.

panels.spec/vizConfig/spec/options/content: Timeouts and Errors - Coordinator

panels.spec/description: Requests that Scylla tried to write but timed out. Timeouts are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.

Requests that Scylla did not even try to write because replicas that were needed to execute this write were unavailable. Unavailable writes are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
Requests that Scylla tried to read but timed out. Timeouts are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.

Requests that Scylla did not even try to read because replicas that were needed to execute this write were unavailable. Unavailable reads are counted in the node that received the request (the coordinator), not at the replicas.
The number of currently active read operations
number of currently queued read operations

panels.spec/title: Writes currently blocked on dirty $func by [[by]]
panels.spec/description: The current number of requests blocked due to reaching the memory quota. Non-zero value indicates that our bottleneck is memory

panels.spec/title: Writes currently blocked on commitlog $func by [[by]]
panels.spec/description: number of currently pending allocations. A non-zero value indicates that we have a bottleneck in the disk write flow.

Reciprocal Miss Rate is a score in the range of 1 to 100 that is used to decide the fraction of read requests to send to each replica - a replica with twice the RMR value of another replica will serve twice the number of read requests.

RMR is calculated on a table level, this is an aggregate estimation of that score.
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], clamp_max(1 + sum((rate(scylla_cache_reads{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]) - rate(scylla_cache_reads_with_misses{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval]))) by ([[by]])/(sum(rate(scylla_cache_reads_with_misses{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) + 0.00001),100))

$topbottom([[filter_limit]], sum(caswlatencya{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^", by="[[by]]", scheduling_group_name=~"$sg"}) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name) or on([[by]], scheduling_group_name) ($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_sum{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name)/($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_write_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]], scheduling_group_name) + 1)))

panels.spec/description: A single Read/Write LWT will result in multiple paxos operations
How many paxos operations that did not yet produce a result are running
How many paxos operations are still running after a result was already returned

An LWT INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE command that involves a condition will be rejected if the condition is not met.

While it is ok, a high value may indicate that there is a potential problem with data distribution

Number of times some INSERT, UPDATE or DELETE request with conditions had to retry because there was a concurrent conditional statement against the same key. Each retry is performed after a randomized sleep interval, so it can lead to statement timing out completely.

It can indicates contention over a hot row or key

Number of times some SELECT with SERIAL consistency had to retry because there was a concurrent conditional statement against the same key. Each retry is performed after a randomized sleep interval, so it can lead to statement timing out completely.

It can indicates contention over a hot row or key
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_contention_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) - $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_contention_bucket{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^", le="1.000000"}[$__rate_interval]))  by ([[by]]))

panels.spec/title: LWT Write Timeout Due to Uncertainty $func by [[by]]
panels.spec/description: Number of partially succeeded conditional statements. These statements were not committed by the coordinator, due to some replicas responding with errors or timing out. The coordinator had to propagate the error to the client. However, the statement succeeded on a minority of replicas, so may later be propagated to the rest during repair.
Number of times a INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE with conditions failed after being unable to contact enough replicas to match the consistency level
Number of times a SELECT with SERIAL consistency failed after being unable to contact enough replicas to match the consistency level

panels.spec/title: LWT Write Unfinished - Repair Attempts $func by [[by]]
panels.spec/description:
Number of Paxos-repairs of INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE with conditions.

A repair is necessary when a previous Paxos statement was partially successful. A subsequent statement then may not proceed before completing the work of its predecessor. A repair is not guaranteed to succeed, the metric indicates the number of repair attempts made

panels.spec/title: LWT Read Unfinished - Repair Attempts $func by [[by]]
panels.spec/description:
Number of Paxos-repairs of SELECT statement with SERIAL consistency.

A repair is necessary when a previous Paxos statement was partially successful. A subsequent statement then may not proceed before completing the work of its predecessor. A repair is not guaranteed to succeed, the metric indicates the number of repair attempts made

Normally, a PREPARE Paxos-round piggy-backs the previous value along with the PREPARE response. When the coordinator is unable to obtain the previous value (or its digest) from some of the participants, or when the digests did not match, a separate repair round has to be performed.

This indicates that some Paxos queries did not run successfully to completion, e.g. because some node is overloaded, down, or there was contention around a key.

Number of pruning requests.

A successful conditional statement deletes the intermediate state from system.paxos table using PRUNE command.

Number of Dropped pruning requests.

A successful conditional statement deletes the intermediate state from system.paxos table using PRUNE command. If the system is busy it may not keep up with the PRUNE requests, so such requests are dropped.

High value suggests the system is overloaded and also that system.paxos table is taking up space. If a prune is dropped, system.paxos table key and value for respective LWT transaction  will stay around until next transaction against the same key or until the gc_grace_period, when it's removed by compaction.
The rate of failed CDC operations.
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(rate(scylla_cdc_operations_failed{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]|$^"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]])/($func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_summary_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) or on ([[by]]) $func(rate(scylla_storage_proxy_coordinator_cas_read_latency_count{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}[$__rate_interval])) by ([[by]]) + 1))

panels.spec/description:
Bloom filter should take a small percentage of the total memory.

scylla_sstables_bloom_filter_memory_size
panels.spec/data/spec/queries/0/spec/query/spec: $topbottom([[filter_limit]], $func(scylla_sstables_bloom_filter_memory_size{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) by ([[by]])/$func(scylla_memory_total_memory{instance=~"[[node]]",cluster="$cluster", dc=~"$dc", shard=~"[[shard]]"}) by ([[by]]))

panels.spec/description: Percentage of CPU time used by compaction

the sum of normalized compaction backlog for all tables in the system.

Backlog is normalized by dividing backlog by shard's available memory.

panels.spec/description:
This is a ballpark estimation of the write-messages size (like insert and update).

It is based on the assumption that write-messages are responsible for most inwards traffic.

panels.spec/description:

It is based on the assumption that read-messages are responsible for most outbound traffic.

spec.label: by
